title: Conjecture 1.2 (Gyárfás)
statement_text: For all integers $k, \ell > 0$ there exists $n(k, \ell)$ such that every graph $G$ with no clique of cardinality more than $k$ and no hole of length more than $\ell$ has chromatic number at most $n(k, \ell)$.
context_text: In 1985, Gyárfás made three conjectures about chromatic number bounds for graphs with restricted clique and hole structure. Conjecture 1.2 concerns graphs with no long holes and no large clique. This paper's main result (Theorem 1.4) settles this conjecture in full.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture completely: for all integers $k, \ell > 0$ there exists $c$ such that every graph $G$ with $\omega(G) \leq k$ and no hole of length more than $\ell$ satisfies $\chi(G) \leq c$.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. III. Long holes

title: Conjecture 1.3 (Gyárfás)
statement_text: For all integers $k, \ell > 0$ there exists $n(k, \ell)$ such that every graph $G$ with no clique of cardinality more than $k$ and no odd hole of length more than $\ell$ has chromatic number at most $n(k, \ell)$.
context_text: This is the third and strongest of Gyárfás's 1985 conjectures; it implies both Conjecture 1.1 and Conjecture 1.2 and remains open at the time of this paper. The authors remark that a variant of their multicover technique can be adapted to the odd-hole setting but omit the details.
paper_contribution: Remarks that the multicover technique can be adapted to prove an analogue of the key lemmas (2.1 and 2.4) under the weaker assumption of no odd hole of length at least $\ell$, suggesting a route toward Conjecture 1.3, but does not formally prove the full conjecture.
notes: PDF source; text is truncated after section 3 begins, so any further conjectures or open questions in later sections are not captured.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. III. Long holes
